phrase: Call `agr_curation_query` before returning any disease term, DOID, synonym, definition, or ontology type.
synonyms: call at least one of agr_curation_query before final output
reason: DISV-RTC / DISV-03 de-dup. The pre-rewrite <success_criteria> restated the required-tool-call imperative that the locked Generated Runtime Contract already injects ('Required tool-call policy: call at least one of agr_curation_query before final output'). The lean rewrite drops the base imperative and folds the curator-facing rule into <goal> once ('call it before reporting any disease term, DOID, name, definition, synonym, or ontology type'); the machine imperative survives in the core half of the assembled render. The literal `agr_curation_query` token is still present in the base prompt.

phrase: return one root JSON object matching `DiseaseValidationResult`. Return only a `DiseaseValidationResult` JSON object with the shared fields at the root.
synonyms: produce JSON matching DiseaseValidationResult; the structured-output layer below is authoritative for final response shape
reason: DISV-OUT / DISV-02 output-mandate de-dup. The pre-rewrite <role> and <output_contract> carried the 'return one root JSON object matching DiseaseValidationResult' / 'Return only a DiseaseValidationResult JSON object' mandate that the locked core already injects ('Output contract from agent.yaml: produce JSON matching DiseaseValidationResult; the structured-output layer below is authoritative for final response shape' plus the CRITICAL structured-output block 'Your final response MUST be valid JSON matching the DiseaseValidationResult schema EXACTLY'). The lean rewrite no longer restates the JSON-only output mandate; the core half of the render owns it. The home check is satisfied by the synonym (the core's injected output-contract line); the disease root-field detail is KEPT in <result_contract> because the core does not enumerate it. The `DiseaseValidationResult` token and the curator-facing 'Do not wrap the object under `validation_result`, `result`, or any other property' rule are both still present in the base prompt.
